title: Living in a college hostel
description: If you are a student staying in a hostel for most of the year, that hostel address qualifies as your ordinary place of residence. You can register from there.
officialBasis: Form 6 Guidelines – ECI: Students may enroll either at their parents' address or the place where they ordinarily reside (hostel/PG).

title: Recently moved to a new city
description: If you've moved to a new city and plan to stay there, you should register in your new constituency. You'll need address proof for the new location.
officialBasis: Form 6 Guidelines – ECI: A person who has shifted from one constituency to another should apply for fresh enrollment in the new constituency.

title: Staying in a rented apartment / PG
description: A rented flat or paying-guest accommodation counts as your ordinary residence. You can use a rent agreement or a utility bill as address proof.
officialBasis: Form 6 Guidelines – ECI: Rent agreement, utility bills, or bank passbook reflecting the address are acceptable proofs.

title: Working in a different state
description: If you're employed in a state different from your home state and live there for most of the year, that work location is your ordinary residence.
officialBasis: Form 6 Guidelines – ECI: 'Ordinary residence' means the place where you actually reside, not necessarily your native place.

title: Living with relatives temporarily
description: If you're staying with relatives temporarily (e.g., during a job search), your original home may still be your ordinary residence. Register where you plan to live long-term.
officialBasis: Form 6 Guidelines – ECI: Temporary stays do not automatically change your ordinary residence.
